Name: Merciless Subjugator
Summary: Opening caster that front-loads party damage with chains and an absorb.
Description:
[Pull Setup] Loosely spread before Chains of Subjugation so the group is not clipped by extra overlap, then use snare breaks or defensives as needed.
[Healer] Clear Leeching Void absorbs quickly before the next burst lands.
[Priority] Kill these first in the opener because they are immune to CC and do the most immediate party damage.

Name: Rift Warden
Summary: Static mini-boss trash that must be fought near its rift while pulsing dangerous void damage.
Description:
[Positioning] Fight the mob within range of its rift so Stabilize does not trigger a lethal explosion.
[Healer] Be ready for Void Expulsion and move out of the circles it leaves behind.
[Utility] Dispel Rift Essence when possible or have the target use a defensive.

Name: Dire Voidbender
Summary: Caster whose buff cast can turn nearby trash into a lethal pull.
Description:
[Kick] Interrupt every Abyssal Enhancement cast.
[Purge] If a cast slips through, immediately purge the buff instead of trying to outheal it.
[Routing] Keep this mob near the front of your interrupt order whenever it is in a pack.

Name: Void-Infused Destroyer
Summary: Large add with targeted bursts and a random frontal that punishes loose positioning.
Description:
[Spread] Eruption picks two players, so avoid stacking and do not clip allies with the follow-up DoT.
[Dodge] Watch for Void Cleave aimed at a random target and sidestep early.
[Defensive] Use personals when Eruption overlaps with other pack damage.

Name: Grand Shadow-Weaver
Summary: Void caster that floods the lane with wave projectiles and single-target pressure.
Description:
[Movement] Avoid Umbral Waves coming from the Gate of the Abyss the mob creates.
[Defensive] Use a personal or combat drop if you are targeted by Void Infusion.
[Priority] Pull carefully around this mob because the lane gets crowded fast once the gate is active.
